title: Pisum vernum
taxonomy: Pisum vernum is classified within the plant family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. This classification is due to its characteristics, such as compound leaves and flowers that produce seed pods. Within the genus Pisum, Pisum vernum is a distinct species that is closely related to other pea species. The exact classification of Pisum vernum may also depend on the specific botanical or taxonomic system being used. However, the Fabaceae family is widely accepted as the correct classification for Pisum vernum.
uses: Pisum vernum, also known as the spring pea, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Its leaves, in particular, are rich in antioxidants and have been used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's seeds are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate pain and swelling. In addition, the plant is believed to have antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and preventing infection. More research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Pisum vernum for medicinal use.

constituents: Pisum vernum is a lesser-known species of the pea family, and research into its active constituents is limited. parts: Pisum vernum is a perennial legume native to Northern Europe and Asia, and it has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
